steps.title: Spot the key contrast signal
steps.content: To contrast the compounds, the answer must highlight what's different about them. The notes show that dichloromethane (an HCC) contains hydrogen, chlorine, and carbon, while tetradecafluorohexane (a PFC) contains fluorine and carbon—the elemental difference that distinguishes them.

steps.title: Find the choice that creates the contrast
steps.content: Choice A presents the two compounds' compositions in parallel form, exposing their elemental differences. Choices B, C, and D either stress what the compounds share or mention only one compound. That matches choice A — A is correct.
